rulings.Case Title: EST. OF WAI FUND DER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues to be cleared/addressed: (1) It appears that two waivers of account are required: (1) Jack Jair needs to waive the account of the successor personal representative (Keeler), and (2) the successor personal representative of the estate of Raymond Jair (Keeler) needs to waive the account of the resigned or removed executor, Jack Jair. This petition includes Paul Perdue's waiver of account on behalf of Mabel Der, who is not a beneficiary in this estate. She may take in Raymond Jair's estate. (2) Need allegation regarding under which authority the court can approve the distribution to the Raymond Jair 1988 Trust, bypassing the distribution to the personal representative of the estate of Raymond Jair, especially since his will is not a pour-over will. (3) The Court may inquire about the assets subject to the administration of the estate of Der Hong (#216027). The Inventory & Appraisal filed in 1979 does not indicate whether the assets are the decedent's separate property or a community property interest. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: EST. OF ANNA MARIA FRANCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/26/26. Examiner's Notes emailed to counsel with the following issues: 1) The verification to the supplement filed on 12/1/25 states that the attorney verified the petition on behalf of the petitioner. Please provide legal authority for counsel to do this. 2) Petitioner alleges in that supplement that the shares of Bank of America stock are variously titled. Please explain how they are currently titled, why they are being re-titled, and by what authority they are being re-titled. Note: if the stocks are not in the name of the decedent, they are not presumptively estate assets. Please also confirm that no distribution of these stocks will be made until final distribution is ordered. 3) The attorney's cost was alleged to be $802.40. The latest proposed order states that it exceeds $1,000. The order must match the petition and will be changed to conform to the petition.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF KIM SENG TANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/13/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A fourth "supplement" was filed on 11/24/25, when an AMENDMENT was required. (Paragraph 1 of the fourth supplement even says the conservator "hereby amends" the accounting.) (2) The 1,961.254 units of T. Rowe Price were appraised at $184,593.23. If 208,782 units were sold with a CV of $18,178.71, and 83.426 units were sold with a CV of $7,263.93, why do the remaining 1,669.046 units have a CV of just $137,902.17? (A similar issue occurs with the Vanguard account.) A fully amended accounting petition must be filed. After the amended accounting petition is e-filed, notice must be given. Deadline to provide the assigned examiner courtesy copies of the amended petition and proof of service is 3/30/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LUCY STOFLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/6/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Petitions for probate of lost wills must clearly state on their face that the will is lost and both the published and mailed notice must so state. [LRSF 14.20.] (2) The petition is not properly verified by the petitioner. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues* to the assigned examiner by 3/23/26. *Either a fully amended petition or a supplement to this petition, signed and verified by the petitioner, clearly indicating that the will is LOST, and proof of mailed and published notice, both showing that the petitioner seeks to admit a LOST will. If a fully amended petition is filed, notice may not be mailed until after the amended petition is e-filed. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: ADAM LI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF DEATH
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues to be cleared/addressed: (1) No proposed order (VS-109, Page 1 only) must be submitted. (2) The Court may inquire further information, such as any efforts to locate the missing person because the petition does not seem to provide sufficient information for the Court to establish the fact of death. A copy of the police incident report alone appears insufficient. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
